task: T54 — Movement/prediction breadth: full MOVEVARS replication, per-client physics presets (g_physics_clientselect), and the sentcvar/sendcvar client-cvar replication channel, with a single wave-wide protocol bump and reserved insertion points for T53 (mode-stats block) and T57 (accuracy fields).
baseBehaviors: stats.qh MOVEVARS catalogue (Base/data/xonotic-data.pk3dir/qcsrc/common/stats.qh:234-448) — exactly 44 REGISTER_STAT(MOVEVARS_*): JUMPSTEP(:234, INT, cvar("sv_jumpstep") — DP engine default 0, xonotic-server.cfg:119 sets 1), FRICTION(:241), FRICTION_SLICK(:242), FRICTION_ONLAND(:243, autocvar_sv_friction_on_land, physicsX.cfg:50=0), JUMPSPEEDCAP_DISABLE_ONRAMPS(:245), JUMPSPEEDCAP_MIN(:246), JUMPSPEEDCAP_MAX(:247), TRACK_CANJUMP(:248), CL_TRACK_CANJUMP(:250 — a cvar LOOPBACK: the client's cl_movement_track_canjump replicated client→server, not a server physics var), HIGHSPEED(:313), AIRACCEL_QW_STRETCHFACTOR(:389), AIRCONTROL_PENALTY(:390), AIRSPEEDLIMIT_NONQW(:391), AIRSTRAFEACCEL_QW(:392), AIRCONTROL_FLAGS(:393), AIRCONTROL_POWER(:394), WARSOWBUNNY_AIRFORWARDACCEL/_ACCEL/_TOPSPEED/_TURNACCEL/_BACKTOSIDERATIO(:405-409), AIRSTOPACCELERATE(:411), AIRSTOPACCELERATE_FULL(:412), AIRSTRAFEACCELERATE(:413), MAXAIRSTRAFESPEED(:414), AIRCONTROL(:415), WALLFRICTION(:426, autocvar_sv_wallfriction), TICRATE(:427, sys_ticrate), TIMESCALE(:428, slowmo), GRAVITY(:429, per-PLAYER), WORLDGRAVITY(:430, sv_gravity global, 'CSQC projectiles etc'), STOPSPEED(:431), MAXSPEED(:432), ACCELERATE(:433), AIRACCELERATE(:434), SLICKACCELERATE(:435), ENTGRAVITY(:438, per-ENTITY (this.gravity)?:1), JUMPVELOCITY(:439), JUMPVELOCITY_CROUCH(:440), MAXAIRSPEED(:441), STEPHEIGHT(:442), AIRACCEL_QW(:443), AIRACCEL_SIDEWAYS_FRICTION(:444), WALLCLIP(:448, autocvar_sv_wallclip — no cfg sets it, default 0). Related non-MOVEVARS movement stats: NOSTEP(:235, cvar sv_nostep), GAMEPLAYFIX_STEPDOWN(:222 — per-player via Physics_ClientOption), GAMEPLAYFIX_STEPDOWN_MAXSPEED(:223 global, physicsX.cfg:60=400), SLICK_APPLYGRAVITY(:365, default 0), DOUBLEJUMP (set per-player at player.qc:141), MOVEFLAGS(:400-403).

Per-player vs global split (qcsrc/common/physics/player.qc Physics_UpdateStats:44-151): every stat WITHOUT a cvar expression in stats.qh is written per-player here through Physics_ClientOption — i.e. the 33 preset-resolvable MOVEVARS (maxspeed:51, airaccel_qw/airstrafeaccel_qw/airspeedlimit_nonqw:54-65, gravity:110, airaccel_qw_stretchfactor:114, maxairstrafespeed:115, maxairspeed:117-119, airstrafeaccelerate:121, warsowbunny_turnaccel:122, airaccel_sideways_friction:123, aircontrol:124, aircontrol_flags:125, aircontrol_power:126, aircontrol_penalty:127, warsowbunny_airforwardaccel:128, warsowbunny_topspeed:129, warsowbunny_accel:130, warsowbunny_backtosideratio:131, friction:132, friction_slick:133, stepheight:134, accelerate:135, stopspeed:136, airaccelerate:137, airstopaccelerate:138, airstopaccelerate_full:139, slickaccelerate:140, jumpvelocity:142, jumpvelocity_crouch:143, jumpspeedcap_max_disable_on_ramps:144, jumpspeedcap_min:145, jumpspeedcap_max:146, track_canjump:147) PLUS DOUBLEJUMP:141 and GAMEPLAYFIX_STEPDOWN:148. Stats WITH a cvar expression (JUMPSTEP, FRICTION_ONLAND, WALLFRICTION, TICRATE, TIMESCALE, WORLDGRAVITY, WALLCLIP, NOSTEP, STEPDOWN_MAXSPEED, SLICK_APPLYGRAVITY) are global — same value to every client, no preset resolution.

maxspd_mod folding + write order (player.qc:46-65,110-119): STAT(MOVEVARS_HIGHSPEED)=autocvar_g_movement_highspeed is written FIRST (:47, xonotic-server.cfg:586 default 1); then MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(PlayerPhysics_UpdateStats) (:49); then maxspd_mod = PHYS_HIGHSPEED(this) * swampslug_slowdown (:50). MAXSPEED = ClientOption(maxspeed)*maxspd_mod (:51, 'also slow walking'). If !g_movement_highspeed_q3_compat (cfg:587 default 0): AIRACCEL_QW = AdjustAirAccelQW(opt, maxspd_mod); AIRSTRAFEACCEL_QW = opt ? AdjustAirAccelQW(opt, mod) : 0; AIRSPEEDLIMIT_NONQW = opt*mod (:60-64); MAXAIRSPEED is NOT scaled (:119). If q3_compat: the three QW vars stay raw and MAXAIRSPEED *= maxspd_mod instead (:54-57,116-117). PostUpdateStats hook last (:150).

Physics_ClientOption (player.qc:18-42) + Physics_Valid (:18-21): if !g_physics_clientselect → defaultval (:25-26). Physics_Valid(s) = s!="" && s!="default" && strhasword(g_physics_clientselect_options, s). Chain: IS_REAL_CLIENT && Physics_Valid(CS_CVAR(this).cvar_cl_physics) → s="g_physics_<set>_<option>"; if cvar_type(s)&CVAR_TYPEFLAG_EXISTS → cvar(s) (:28-33). Else if g_physics_clientselect_default != ""/"default" → "g_physics_<default>_<option>" if EXISTS (deliberately NOT Physics_Valid-checked, comment :36 — the default can be forced to an unlisted set) (:34-40). Else defaultval (the sv_* autocvar). Shipped cvars (physics.cfg:9-11): g_physics_clientselect 0, g_physics_clientselect_options "xonotic nexuiz vecxis quake quake2 quake3 cpma bones xdf", g_physics_clientselect_default "". Each preset block defines exactly 36 vars (physics.cfg:16-51 for xonotic): gravity, airaccel_qw, airstrafeaccel_qw, airspeedlimit_nonqw, maxspeed, jumpvelocity, jumpvelocity_crouch, maxairstrafespeed, maxairspeed, airstrafeaccelerate, warsowbunny_turnaccel, airaccel_qw_stretchfactor, airaccel_sideways_friction, aircontrol, aircontrol_flags, aircontrol_power, aircontrol_penalty, warsowbunny_airforwardaccel, warsowbunny_topspeed, warsowbunny_accel, warsowbunny_backtosideratio, friction, friction_slick, stepheight, accelerate, stopspeed, airaccelerate, airstopaccelerate, airstopaccelerate_full, slickaccelerate, doublejump, jumpspeedcap_min, jumpspeedcap_max, jumpspeedcap_max_disable_on_ramps, track_canjump, stepdown. NOTE jumpspeedcap_min/max use the literal string "nan" to mean 'disabled' (xdf/quake3/cpma set REAL 0 — 0 and nan are distinct values).

REPLICATE machinery (qcsrc/lib/replicate.qh): CSQC side — ReplicateVars polled with period 0.8 + random()*0.4 s (:43-49); ReplicateVars_Start sends ALL once at init (REPLICATEVARS_SEND_ALL, :50-57); per-cvar check 'value != last' → ReplicateVars_Send → localcmd "cl_cmd sendcvar <name>" (:102-123). SVQC side (REPLICATE_7 :79-100): f==0 (request) → stuffcmd(this, "cl_cmd sendcvar <var>") (:95); f>0 (receive) → parse argv(i+1) into the per-client field on the clientstate store entity (string variant strcpy + an optional fixup func run on the RECEIVED value, :63-73 'also initialize to the default value of func'); f<0 destroy. Client transmit (qcsrc/client/command/cl_cmd.qc LocalCommand_sendcvar:395-428): s = cvar_string(name); cl_weaponpriority → W_FixWeaponOrder(W_NumberWeaponOrder(s),1), cl_weaponpriorityN → W_FixWeaponOrder(...,0) (:408-411); then localcmd("cmd sentcvar <name> \"<s>\"") (:413). Server receive (qcsrc/server/command/cmd.qc ClientCommand_sentcvar:804-836): argc>=3 → GetCvars(caller, CS_CVAR(caller), 1); registered as CLIENT_COMMAND(sentcvar) (:1125) and exempted from flood limits (:1200). GetCvars (qcsrc/server/command/getreplies.qc:387-406): f=-1 cleanup / 0 request (now deprecated, warning :392 — clients push via REPLICATE) / 1 receive; name=argv(f), value=argv(f+1); dispatches through Notification_GetCvars + ReplicateVars + ReplicateVars_ApplyChange.

Replicated cvar inventory (grep REPLICATE( over qcsrc): common/replicate.qh:15-23 — cl_autoswitch(bool, default 1 commands.cfg:158), cl_autoscreenshot(int), cl_clippedspectating(bool), cl_autoswitch_cts(int, -1 xonotic-client.cfg:844), cl_handicap(VECTOR), cl_handicap_damage_given/taken(float), cl_noantilag(bool, 0 xonotic-client.cfg:918), g_xonoticversion(string). physics/player.qc:7-9 — cl_physics(string, "default" xonotic-client.cfg:1049), cl_jetpack_jump(bool, 1 :835), cl_movement_track_canjump(bool, 0 :247). weapons/all.qc:799-818 — cl_accuracy_data_share/receive, cl_gunalign(int), cl_weapon_switch_reload, cl_weapon_switch_fallback_to_impulse, cl_weaponimpulsemode, cl_weaponpriority(string, default xonotic-client.cfg:796, fixup W_FixWeaponOrder_ForceComplete_AndBuildImpulseList), cl_weaponpriority0..9 (fixup AllowIncomplete). Plus mutator-owned: cl_dodging(+_timeout), cl_multijump, cl_nade_type/cl_pokenade_type, cl_spawn_near_teammate, cl_buffs_autoreplace, cl_casings, r_drawviewmodel, cl_autotaunt, cl_voice_directional(+_taunt_attenuation), cl_allow_uid2name/uidranking/uidtracking. The QC alias 'autoswitch' (commands.cfg:182) sets the local cvar AND sends 'cmd autoswitch'.

cl_noantilag consumption (server side): qcsrc/server/weapons/tracing.qc:115 'if (antilag && !CS_CVAR(ent).cvar_cl_noantilag)' gates the fireBullet antilag trace; qcsrc/server/antilag.qc:154,205,212,223,230 — noantilag(shooter) → lag forced 0 (no rewind for that shooter). cl_physics consumption: only Physics_ClientOption (player.qc:28-33) + the 'physics' client command. cl_autoswitch consumption: pickup→best-weapon switch. cl_weaponpriority consumption: w_getbestweapon / W_GetCycleWeapon (selection.qh).

Config defaults baseline: xonotic-server.cfg:675 'exec physicsX.cfg' (stock Xonotic set: sv_gravity 800, sv_maxspeed 360, sv_maxairspeed 360, sv_stopspeed 100, sv_accelerate 15, sv_airaccelerate 2, sv_slickaccelerate 15, sv_friction 6, sv_stepheight 31, sv_jumpvelocity 260, sv_airaccel_qw -0.8, sv_airaccel_qw_stretchfactor 2, sv_airstopaccelerate 3, sv_airstrafeaccelerate 18, sv_maxairstrafespeed 100, sv_airstrafeaccel_qw -0.95, sv_aircontrol 100, sv_aircontrol_power 2, sv_airspeedlimit_nonqw 900, sv_warsowbunny_* 0/0.1593/925/0.8, sv_friction_on_land 0, sv_friction_slick 0.5, sv_doublejump 0, sv_jumpspeedcap_min/max "nan", sv_jumpspeedcap_max_disable_on_ramps 1, sv_track_canjump 0, sv_gameplayfix_stepdown 2, sv_gameplayfix_stepdown_maxspeed 400, g_movement_highspeed_q3_compat 0) and :682 'exec physics.cfg' (the full g_physics_<preset>_* table — so preset cvars EXIST in any store loaded from xonotic-server.cfg). g_movement_highspeed 1 (xonotic-server.cfg:586).
portState: MoveVarsBlock (XonoticGodot/src/XonoticGodot.Net/MoveVarsBlock.cs) already replicates 40 cvars, NOT 8 (:25-39): sv_maxspeed/accelerate/friction/friction_slick/stopspeed/slickaccelerate/friction_on_land, sv_maxairspeed/airaccelerate/airaccel_qw/airstrafeaccel_qw/airaccel_qw_stretchfactor/airspeedlimit_nonqw/airaccel_sideways_friction/maxairstrafespeed/airstrafeaccelerate/airstopaccelerate/airstopaccelerate_full, sv_aircontrol(+flags/power/penalty), sv_warsowbunny_* (5), sv_jumpvelocity(+_crouch), sv_jumpspeedcap_min/max(+disable_on_ramps), sv_track_canjump, sv_doublejump, sv_jumpstep, sv_gravity, sv_stepheight, sv_gameplayfix_stepdown(+_maxspeed), sv_wallfriction. Positional float vector w/ leading count byte (:62-78), FNV-1a content hash (:81-90). The '8 movevars' in the task brief is the PlayerState stat subset (src/XonoticGodot.Net/InputCommand.cs:140-147: MaxSpeed/Accelerate/AirAccelerate/Friction/StopSpeed/JumpVelocity/Gravity/StepHeight) carried by the Reconciler — informational only; the live physics read is ambient.

Replication wiring already live (T12): ServerNet.BroadcastSnapshots captures the GLOBAL block once per tick (game/net/ServerNet.cs:753-755) and sends it per peer only when PeerState.LastMoveVarsHash differs (:779-786, field :148). ClientNet.HandleSnapshot reads bool+block and STAMPS the values into the client cvar store via MoveVarsBlock.Apply(Api.Cvars, mv) then refreshes _vars=VarsFromCvars() (game/net/ClientNet.cs:486-494, :751). The predictor's live read is PlayerPhysics.Move → MovementParameters.FromCvars() each tick (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Physics/PlayerPhysics.cs:110), shared verbatim by server sim and client prediction (EntityMovementStep → Movement.Move, game/net/EntityMovementStep.cs:29-80, which sets input.Predicted=true :62).

MovementParameters (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Physics/MovementParameters.cs) already models the FULL Xonotic param set incl. hull (:17-76), stock Defaults (:150-204), FromCvars with unset-fallback helpers Cvar/CvarRaw/CvarBool/CvarNan (:84-144, :226-256), and ApplyHighSpeed matching QC's non-q3compat maxspd_mod fold — MaxSpeed/AirSpeedLimitNonQW/MaxAirStrafeSpeed scaled + AdjustAirAccelQW on the two QW fractions (:212-221). GAPS: (a) no preset (g_physics_<set>_*) resolution anywhere; (b) g_movement_highspeed cvar is read NOWHERE in src/ — PlayerPhysics seeds player.SpeedMultiplier=1 unconditionally (PlayerPhysics.cs:156) so a server-set g_movement_highspeed≠1 is ignored; (c) g_movement_highspeed_q3_compat not modeled; (d) sv_wallclip/sv_nostep/sv_slick_applygravity not replicated (slick_applygravity is a documented no-op default at PlayerPhysics.cs:308); (e) sv_gameplayfix_nudgeoutofsolid is read ambiently by string (PlayerPhysics.cs:130) but NOT in MoveVarsBlock — a server that disables it desyncs prediction.

Protocol: game/net/NetProtocol.cs ProtocolVersion = 6 (:50), folded into BuildParity (:82-92) and checked at handshake. NetControl ids 1-17 used; next free = 18 (:111-154). Snapshot wire order (ServerNet.cs:770-825 / ClientNet.cs:464-559): [Snapshot byte][serverTime f32][ackedSeq u64][ownerNetId u16][owner state][bool moveVars→block][bool scoreInfo→block][bool scores→block][delta entity section]. Owner state (WriteOwnerState ServerNet.cs:1107-1134 / read ClientNet.cs:471-478): origin v3f, velocity v3f, onGround bool, health i16, armor i16, activeWeaponId i16, respawnTimeStat f32, spectateeStatus i16 — append-at-END lockstep contract (player-loop parity memory).

sentcvar/sendcvar: ABSENT on both sides. Client→server console channel exists: NetControl.ClientCommand/ServerPrint (NetProtocol.cs:144-147), ClientNet.SendStringCommand (~:420-433), ServerNet.HandleClientCommand → _world.Commands.Execute(line, isServerConsole:false, caller:peer.Player) (:412-426). The console routes unknown commands to localRouter (listen) or remoteSender (clc_stringcmd) via ConsoleCommands.RouteUnknown (src/XonoticGodot.Engine/Console/ConsoleCommands.cs:101-104); the menu's 'Apply immediately' button issues the string "sendcvar cl_weaponpriority" (game/menu/dialogs/DialogSettingsGame.cs:679-680) which currently dead-ends as an unknown command. ClientNet hook points for the periodic watcher: Poll (:267-280, Accepted set in HandleAccept :443-452) and SendInput (:328-364).

T56 already landed the related client verbs in src/XonoticGodot.Server/Commands.cs: autoswitch (:364, CmdAutoswitch :1393-1406, per-player Dictionary<Player,bool> _autoswitch :138, getter GetAutoswitch :151), physics (CmdPhysics :1413-1444 — g_physics_clientselect gate, list/valid/default branches faithful, per-player Dictionary<Player,string> _clPhysics :141), clientversion (:1451-1459). There is NO generic per-client cvar table (no CS_CVAR equivalent) and nothing consumes _clPhysics for actual physics. cl_weaponpriority currently reaches weapon logic via Inventory.WeaponPriority(Entity) reading the GLOBAL Api.Cvars store (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Gameplay/Inventory.cs:162-166) — correct only on a listen server for the local player. cl_noantilag: no consumer; ServerNet.BeginLagComp (:1167-1198) rewinds for every shooter unconditionally.

Support already in place: server boot loads the FULL preset table — GameWorld.Boot → ConfigLoader.LoadServerConfig (src/XonoticGodot.Server/GameWorld.cs:334) execs xonotic-server.cfg which execs physics.cfg (:682), so g_physics_<preset>_<var> cvars exist in the server store. CvarService.Has = the CVAR_TYPEFLAG_EXISTS equivalent (src/XonoticGodot.Engine/Simulation/EngineServices.cs:331; note ICvarService at src/XonoticGodot.Common/Services/Services.cs:68-74 has NO exists-check — only the concrete class does). Cvars registry registers g_physics_clientselect + _options but NOT g_physics_clientselect_default (src/XonoticGodot.Server/Cvars.cs:325-327). WeaponOrder fixup helpers are in Common and reachable from Commands.cs (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Gameplay/Weapons/WeaponOrder.cs:30,101,110). DEAD/ABSENT: bot-side cl_physics, cl_handicap, accuracy-share cvars — no consumers yet.

Hazard discovered for any cvar-stamping design: NetGame's shared→server cvar bridge (game/net/NetGame.cs:499-508) mirrors EVERY shared-store change whose name the server store already Has — sv_maxspeed etc. qualify. If the client ever stamps PRESET-RESOLVED values into shared sv_* cvars on a listen host, the bridge would propagate the local player's preset into the authoritative store and change every player's physics. Also CvarService.ParseFloat (EngineServices.cs:372-374, NumberStyles.Float) parses "nan" → 0f fallback, NOT NaN — so jumpspeedcap "nan" preset values must be special-cased by string, and MovementParameters.CvarNan (:252-256) conflates a REAL 0 (xdf/quake3/cpma jumpspeedcap_min 0) with unset→NaN.
plan: 1. Extend MoveVarsBlock.MovementCvars (src/XonoticGodot.Net/MoveVarsBlock.cs:25-39) by APPENDING (never reordering — keep Apply prefix-stable) 6 globals: "g_movement_highspeed" (stock 1), "g_movement_highspeed_q3_compat" (0), "sv_gameplayfix_nudgeoutofsolid" (1 — already read ambiently by PlayerPhysics.cs:130), "sv_wallclip" (0), "sv_nostep" (0), "sv_slick_applygravity" (0). Capture must read g_movement_highspeed with unset→1 semantics (GetString=="" → 1, since GetFloat returns 0 for unset). Document the QC stat each entry mirrors. Explicitly EXCLUDE: MOVEVARS_TICRATE/TIMESCALE (the port advertises tickrate at handshake and ships dt per InputCommand), MOVEVARS_ENTGRAVITY (per-entity, follow-up), MOVEVARS_CL_TRACK_CANJUMP (client→server, rides sentcvar instead).

2. Add MovementParameters.FromValues(float[] values) (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Physics/MovementParameters.cs) building the struct positionally from a MoveVarsBlock vector (same NaN handling as CvarNan but trusting NaN floats on the wire), plus fields/reads for the new cvars (HighSpeed default 1, HighSpeedQ3Compat false, WallClip 0 — doc-only until a consumer lands). Add 'public static MovementParameters? PredictionOverride' (a static, default null) — the client-prediction injection point.

3. New file src/XonoticGodot.Common/Physics/PhysicsPreset.cs — port of Physics_ClientOption/Physics_Valid (player.qc:18-42): Valid(cvars, set) = set!=""/"default" && whitespace-word-contained-in g_physics_clientselect_options; Resolve(cvars, clPhysics, option, fallbackName): clientselect gate → "g_physics_<set>_<option>" if EXISTS → "g_physics_<clientselect_default>_<option>" if EXISTS (no validity check, faithful to :34-40) → cvars.GetFloat(fallbackName). Existence check: prefer the concrete CvarService.Has via an optional Func<string,bool> exists parameter (ServerNet passes _world.Services.CvarsImpl.Has); fall back to GetString!="". Special-case the string "nan" → float.NaN for jumpspeedcap_min/max (CvarService.ParseFloat would yield 0). Expose the 36-option preset table with each option's sv_* fallback name (option 'stepdown'→sv_gameplayfix_stepdown, 'doublejump'→sv_doublejump, rest are sv_<option>); register g_physics_clientselect_default "" in src/XonoticGodot.Server/Cvars.cs (next to :326-327).

4. Per-client physics on the SERVER: in ServerNet.BroadcastSnapshots (game/net/ServerNet.cs:753-786) keep the existing global capture, and additionally per peer compute the preset-RESOLVED vector when g_physics_clientselect!=0 and the peer's replicated cl_physics is valid: same 46-name MovementCvars order, preset-resolvable entries resolved via PhysicsPreset.Resolve, the rest copied from the global vector. Wire: immediately AFTER the existing movevars bool/block write a second bool 'sendResolved' (hash-gated per peer via a new PeerState.LastResolvedVarsHash; always false when no preset active) + MoveVarsBlock.Serialize of the resolved vector. Server-side authority application: in PlayerPhysics.Move:110 replace 'MovementParameters.FromCvars()' with a resolver-aware read — 'mp = MovementParameters.Resolve(player, input.Predicted)' where Resolve = (Predicted && PredictionOverride!=null) ? PredictionOverride : (PresetProvider?.Invoke(player) is float[] v ? FromValues(v) : FromCvars()). PresetProvider is a static Func<Entity,float[]?> set by ServerNet (per-tick cache of each player's resolved vector; null when clientselect off → today's exact path). Also fix PlayerPhysics.cs:156: seed player.SpeedMultiplier from g_movement_highspeed (unset/0 → 1) instead of the literal 1 — mirrors Physics_UpdateStats:47/:50 with the fold still happening at ApplyHighSpeed (:171), identical on both sides because the cvar is now replicated (step 1).

5. Per-client physics on the CLIENT: ClientNet.HandleSnapshot (game/net/ClientNet.cs:486-494) — after the existing global Apply, read the new resolved bool/block; when present set MovementParameters.PredictionOverride = FromValues(resolved) (+ _vars = ToPlayerVars(it)); when bool false AND a 'preset cleared' sentinel (send the block once with count 0 on preset deactivation, or simply re-send with hash of the global vector) clear the override. NEVER stamp resolved per-player values into the cvar store (the NetGame.cs:499-508 bridge would leak them into the authoritative store on a listen host). Default path (clientselect 0, stock) = override stays null = byte-identical behavior.

6. sentcvar server side (src/XonoticGodot.Server/Commands.cs — owned): add a per-client cvar table Dictionary<Player, Dictionary<string,string>> _clientCvars + getters GetClientCvar(Player,name,fallback)/GetClientCvarBool/Float, cleaned in the existing player-forget path. Register("sentcvar", ...): require ctx.Caller (privilege separation — NEVER write the global store; T47), require ArgCount>=3 (QC :810), allowlist initial set {cl_weaponpriority, cl_weaponpriority0..9, cl_autoswitch, cl_autoswitch_cts, cl_noantilag, cl_physics, cl_movement_track_canjump, cl_jetpack_jump} (silently ignore others — QC stores only known REPLICATE fields). Apply QC's server-side fixups on receive (replicate.qh:63-73): cl_weaponpriority → WeaponOrder.FixWeaponOrderForceComplete; cl_weaponpriorityN → FixWeaponOrder(.., complete:false). Bridge into the existing T56 state: cl_autoswitch also writes _autoswitch; cl_physics validated via PhysicsPreset.Valid then also writes _clPhysics (one source of truth — migrate CmdPhysics to write the table too). No success output (QC prints nothing on the happy path); usage string on short args ('Usage:^3 cmd sentcvar <cvar> <arguments>', cmd.qc:831-832).

7. Consumers: (a) Inventory.WeaponPriority — add 'public static Func<Entity,string?>? PriorityProvider' consulted before the global cvar read (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Gameplay/Inventory.cs:162-166, additive); Commands ctor (owned) wires it to the per-client table (Player → GetClientCvar(p, "cl_weaponpriority", "")). (b) cl_noantilag — in ServerNet.BeginLagComp (game/net/ServerNet.cs:1167-1177, owned) skip the rewind when _world.Commands.GetClientCvarBool(sp, "cl_noantilag") (antilag.qc:154 parity; per-shooter, the restore path untouched). (c) cl_physics — consumed by step 4's per-peer resolution (read each tick from the table so a mid-match 'cmd physics cpma' or sentcvar update re-resolves next snapshot via the hash gate).

8. sendcvar client side: (a) register a 'sendcvar <cvar>' builtin in ConsoleCommands (src/XonoticGodot.Engine/Console/ConsoleCommands.cs Register():67-105) — reads _cvars.GetString, routes the line 'sentcvar <name> "<value>"' through the existing RouteUnknown path (localRouter on a listen host with caller, else remoteSender) — this makes the menu's 'Apply immediately' button (DialogSettingsGame.cs:680) and the QC menu's sendcvar usage work. The cl_weaponpriority client-side fixup may call WeaponOrder (Common, referenced by Engine) or be skipped (server-side fixup from step 6 + GetCycleWeapon's skipMissing make it tolerant). (b) automatic replication (lib/replicate.qh parity): in ClientNet (owned) add a ReplicatedCvars watcher — string[] {cl_weaponpriority, cl_autoswitch, cl_noantilag, cl_physics} initially; on Accepted (HandleAccept :443-452) send ALL (ReplicateVars_Start), then poll every 0.8+0.4·random s (piggyback on SendInput's clock :328) comparing against a last-sent cache and sending changed ones via SendStringCommand. Cvar source = 'public Func<string,string>? ReplicatedCvarSource' (NetGame wires it to the shared menu/console store where cl_* live; default falls back to Api.Cvars). Register defaults if absent: cl_autoswitch "1" (commands.cfg:158), cl_noantilag "0" (xonotic-client.cfg:918), cl_physics "default" (:1049), cl_weaponpriority = the shipped list (xonotic-client.cfg:796).

9. chokepointSnippets: GameWorld.cs / DamageSystem.cs / MapObjectsRegistry.cs / Registries.cs — NO changes required by T54. (Per-client state lives in Commands.cs which T54 owns; preset resolution reads the server store ServerNet already holds via _world.Services.Cvars/CvarsImpl; GameWorld.Boot already loads physics.cfg through LoadServerConfig at GameWorld.cs:334.)

FOR T53 (mode-stats block) — insertion point T54 reserves in files T54 owns: ServerNet.BroadcastSnapshots, AFTER the scores block write (game/net/ServerNet.cs:805-811) and BEFORE st.SnapHistory.EncodeSnapshot (:814): write 'bool sendModeStats' + (when true) the T53 block, hash/version-gated per peer like the movevars pattern (:779-786). Mirror read in ClientNet.HandleSnapshot AFTER the scoreboard read (game/net/ClientNet.cs:519-524) and BEFORE 'if (r.BadRead) return;' (:526) — ALWAYS consume the bytes even with no consumer wired (the ScoreInfo comment :507 documents the alignment contract). T53 hands T54 the two snippets (writer + reader incl. the block's field order) and T54 integrates; covered by the single v7 bump — no extra version change needed.

FOR T57 (accuracy fields) — insertion point: per-owner data goes at the END of the owner state — append after 'w.WriteShort(spec)' in ServerNet.WriteOwnerState (game/net/ServerNet.cs:1133) with the mirrored reads appended after 'SpectateeStatus = r.ReadShort()' (game/net/ClientNet.cs:478). The owner block has NO per-field gating — strict append-at-END lockstep (both sides must land in the same commit). If T57's payload is large/sparse (per-weapon arrays), prefer instead a bool-gated block in the reserved slot directly after T53's mode-stats slot; T57 hands the field list + order, T54 integrates under the same v7 bump.

FOR T39 (or any task adding a new top-level frame kind): next free NetControl id is 18 (game/net/NetProtocol.cs:153 ends at MinigameState=17); hand T54 the enum entry + dispatch case (ServerNet.OnPacket / ClientNet packet switch) and it rides the same v7 bump. Any change to InputCommand.Serialize (src/XonoticGodot.Net/InputCommand.cs:77-105) likewise routes through T54.

NOTE for the orchestrator: ServerNet.cs/ClientNet.cs/NetProtocol.cs/Commands.cs ARE chokepoint files owned by T54 this wave — other tasks must hand snippets for them to T54, per the reserved points above. T54's own out-of-ownership edits are small and in files no other A3 task lists: PlayerPhysics.cs (2 edits), Inventory.cs (additive hook), ConsoleCommands.cs (additive builtin), NetGame.cs (1 wiring line), Cvars.cs (1 registration line).
risks: Listen-server cvar feedback loop: NetGame's shared→server bridge (game/net/NetGame.cs:499-508) forwards ANY shared-store change the server store knows — stamping preset-RESOLVED values into shared sv_* would silently rewrite the authoritative physics for every player. The plan's PredictionOverride avoids the cvar store entirely for per-player values; do NOT 'simplify' back to cvar stamping.

"nan" parsing: CvarService.ParseFloat (EngineServices.cs:372-374) yields 0 for "nan", and MovementParameters.CvarNan (:252-256) maps 0→NaN — which is WRONG for presets that set a real 0 (g_physics_xdf_jumpspeedcap_min 0, quake3/cpma too). PhysicsPreset must read jumpspeedcap_min/max by STRING and map "nan"→NaN, real numbers verbatim; FromValues must pass NaN through untouched (the wire is float, NaN survives serialization — MoveVarsBlock already relies on this).

Golden-trace compatibility: MovementParityTests + tools/movement-ref assume stock defaults. All additions must be default-inert: g_physics_clientselect ships 0 (resolver returns sv_*), PredictionOverride/PresetProvider default null, g_movement_highspeed unset→seed 1 (xonotic-server.cfg:586 value). The SpeedMultiplier seed change (PlayerPhysics.cs:156) is the riskiest — verify a g_movement_highspeed=1 run is bit-identical and the mutator hooks still see seed semantics (powerups MULTIPLY onto the seed; QC PlayerPhysics_UpdateStats hook runs after the seed at player.qc:47-50, same order).

Static ambient state (MovementParameters.PredictionOverride, PresetProvider, Inventory.PriorityProvider) leaks across sequentially-run tests and across listen-server rehosts — clear in ClientNet.Dispose/ServerNet.Dispose and reset in test setup (xUnit parallelism is already off assembly-wide, so no races, but ordering leaks are real).

Speed-powerup/swampslug fold divergence (pre-existing, surfaced by this work): QC replicates maxspd_mod-FOLDED stats per player (player.qc:50-65) so prediction includes the Speed powerup; the port folds at move time via SpeedMultiplier, and the client's prediction carrier may not run the server's mutator state → mispredict while a Speed powerup is held. Out of T54 scope — do not try to fold the multiplier into the replicated block (double-apply); note for a follow-up (replicate the owner's SpeedMultiplier in the owner block tail).

Protocol-bump coordination: a SINGLE 6→7 bump must land with or before T53/T57 snippet integration; if T53/T57 slip to a later wave their reserved slots are just absent (no bytes written) — the reservation is doc-only, not wire padding, so nothing breaks; but if they land, their snippets must come through T54's files to keep one bump.

sentcvar abuse surface: the handler must be caller-gated and allowlisted (write ONLY the per-client table, never Api.Cvars / the world store) — this is exactly the T47 privilege-separation gap class; also clamp value length (the wire string is attacker-controlled) and treat cl_physics through PhysicsPreset.Valid so a hostile preset name can't probe arbitrary 'g_physics_<x>_<y>' cvars beyond the gated chain (faithful QC behavior already constrains lookups to the g_physics_ prefix).

cl_weaponpriority fixup parity: QC fixes the order CLIENT-side before send (cl_cmd.qc:408-411) AND server-side via the REPLICATE fixup. The port plan fixes server-side only (WeaponOrder.FixWeaponOrderForceComplete); confirm FixWeaponOrderForceComplete tolerates Overkill tokens (MenuWeaponOrderTests.cs:91 says ok* tokens are kept) so the round-trip doesn't strip user entries.

ICvarService has no exists-check (Services.cs:68-74): preset resolution via GetString!="" misreads a cvar explicitly set to empty as 'absent'. Preset table values are never empty (physics.cfg) so this is safe — but pass CvarService.Has where the concrete store is available (ServerNet has _world.Services.CvarsImpl) to stay exact.

The deprecated GetCvars f==0 'request' direction (server stuffcmd → client) is intentionally NOT ported (QC itself warns it is deprecated, getreplies.qc:392); the client-push watcher replaces it. A server cannot demand an unlisted cvar — acceptable, but document it in the sentcvar handler comment.
testPlan: dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj && dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests — entire existing suite (1059+, incl. MovementParityTests golden traces and SnapshotDeltaTests) stays green with zero golden-file regeneration (defaults unchanged).

PhysicsPresetTests: (1) clientselect=0 → Resolve returns sv_* fallback for every option; (2) clientselect=1 + cl_physics=cpma → g_physics_cpma_* values (spot-check aircontrol 150, aircontrol_flags 5, accelerate 15, jumpspeedcap_min 0 — REAL zero, not NaN); (3) invalid/unlisted preset name → fallback chain; (4) g_physics_clientselect_default forced to an unlisted set resolves (Physics_Valid deliberately skipped, player.qc:36); (5) "nan" strings → float.NaN; (6) missing g_physics_<set>_<option> cvar falls through the chain.

MoveVarsBlock round-trip (SnapshotDeltaTests pattern :138-156) with the 46-entry list: Capture→Serialize→Deserialize→Apply equality + hash stability + NaN survival; verify g_movement_highspeed captures 1 from an unset store.

Commands sentcvar tests: 'sentcvar cl_weaponpriority "vortex blaster"' (caller=p) → GetClientCvar returns the fixed-up complete order; 'sentcvar cl_autoswitch 0' syncs GetAutoswitch(p)==false; 'sentcvar cl_physics cpma' bridges into the preset table the snapshot capture reads; non-allowlisted name ('sentcvar sv_cheats 1') stores nothing and never touches the world store; null caller rejected; <3 args prints the usage line.

Client watcher test over NetLoopback: on handshake Accept all 4 cvars are sent (ReplicateVars_Start parity); changing cl_weaponpriority in the wired source sends exactly one sentcvar within 1.2 sim-seconds; unchanged cvars are not re-sent.

Prediction-parity integration test: scripted bunny-hop input replayed through EntityMovementStep with PredictionOverride = a cpma-resolved vector vs a GameWorld server running clientselect=1/cl_physics=cpma — origins match within reconcile epsilon every tick (the T54 acceptance criterion); rerun with clientselect=0 and assert byte-identical to the pre-change behavior.
SpeedMultiplier seed test: g_movement_highspeed=2 → effective MaxSpeed 720 and AdjustAirAccelQW fold applied (compare against hand-computed QC values); unset → identical to current outputs.

Manual/headless smoke: 'dotnet ... --host' + second '--connect' client; console: sendcvar cl_weaponpriority (menu Apply button path), set g_physics_clientselect 1, cmd physics list / cmd physics quake3 / cmd physics default; with 'set developer 1' confirm no '[reconcile] origin SNAP' spam after each switch and that the resolved block is re-sent exactly once per change (hash gate log).
